mnemonics: 3-Point Search: Find the Goldilocks point (not too high, not too low) like testing porridge. Fibonacci Search: Use the famous sequence (0,1,1,2,3,5...) to efficiently shrink the interval like a spiraling seashell.
keyPoints: Both methods are used to find the minimum of a unimodal function by iteratively narrowing an interval of uncertainty.
3-Point Search evaluates the function at three equally spaced interior points to eliminate a portion of the interval.

Fibonacci Search uses the Fibonacci number sequence to determine the most efficient non-equal spacing of evaluation points, achieving the greatest possible reduction in the interval with the fewest function evaluations.
commonMistakes: Applying the methods to non-unimodal functions, which can lead to converging on a local minimum instead of the intended global one.
Incorrectly calculating or applying the Fibonacci sequence, leading to inefficient or erroneous interval reduction.
